name: Story DoR — Capital Markets Extensions (Starter Draft)
description: Placeholder criteria for Capital Markets stories. These are starter content drafted to show the pattern — the CapMkts team should review and customize before scoring real stories.
placeholder_note: Drafted by the platform team as a starting point. Capital Markets stewards: review, edit, and remove this banner once you've validated the criteria with your domain experts.

criteria.name: Settlement Window Specified
criteria.description: The expected settlement timing (T+0, T+1, T+2) and its implications on the workflow are stated.
criteria.why: Missing the settlement window leads to systems that pass tests but fail in production trading hours.
criteria.fix: State the settlement window (T+0 / T+1 / T+2) and any market-close cutoffs that constrain the workflow.
criteria.good_example: Settlement: T+0. Cutoff: 14:30 ET (1 hour before market close). Late submissions queue for next-day allocation.

criteria.name: Pricing Source / Curve Identified
criteria.description: The pricing source, curve, or rate engine the story depends on is named with its refresh cadence.
criteria.why: Pricing assumptions baked into stories without an authoritative source cause downstream P&L disputes.
criteria.fix: Name the pricing source (e.g. internal curve engine, vendor feed) and the refresh cadence; confirm the squad has access.
criteria.good_example: Pricing: internal MBS curve, refreshed every 15 minutes from the trading desk model. Source: pricing.mbs_curve_v3.

criteria.name: Counterparty / Dealer Coverage Stated
criteria.description: Which counterparties or dealers the story applies to (and which it explicitly does NOT) is documented.
criteria.why: Implicit counterparty scope leads to either silent under-coverage or surprise integrations mid-sprint.
criteria.fix: List the counterparties / dealers in scope and out of scope.
criteria.good_example: In scope: top 5 dealers by Q1 2026 volume (DealerA, DealerB, DealerC, DealerD, DealerE). Out of scope: Tier 2 dealers — handled in FEAT-CM-073.

criteria.name: Portfolio / Risk Impact Assessed
criteria.description: How the change affects portfolio metrics, hedges, or risk limits is stated and signed off by Risk.
criteria.why: Trading-system changes that pass functional ACs but breach risk limits cause production incidents.
criteria.fix: Document the portfolio / hedge / risk-limit impact. Confirm sign-off by Risk for any non-trivial change.
criteria.good_example: Risk impact: increases convexity exposure on MBS book by ~0.2%. Within current risk limits. Signed off by [Risk lead] on [date].
